items.fix: Standardize or normalize numeric columns (StandardScaler, MinMaxScaler) in a pipeline before fit/predict.
items.why: Distance sums squared differences across features; a column in thousands swamps a column in 0–1, so neighbors match on scale artifacts, not semantics.
items.example: Age (0–100) vs income ($0–$500k) without scaling → k-NN neighbors differ mainly on income digits.

items.mistake: Using even k in binary classification without a tie policy
items.fix: Prefer odd k, or define weighted vote / smallest-distance tie-break when k neighbors split 50/50.
items.why: Even k can produce equal votes for two classes, leaving prediction undefined or arbitrary.
items.example: k = 4 with labels {0, 0, 1, 1} — specify tie-break or use k = 5.

items.fix: Hold out validation/test data or cross-validate; tune k on validation, report final metrics on test.
items.why: k = 1 often achieves high training accuracy by memorizing points but generalizes poorly on noisy data.
items.example: Train acc 1.0 with k = 1, test acc 0.62 → pick larger k or weighted vote after validation sweep.

items.mistake: Ignoring class imbalance in majority vote
items.fix: Use distance-weighted vote, stratified sampling of neighbors, or resampling; choose metrics like F1 or balanced accuracy.
items.why: Global majority class among neighbors reflects dataset skew, not local evidence for the query.
items.example: 99% negative class: k = 11 neighbors often all negative even near a rare positive cluster.

items.mistake: Deploying naive k-NN on millions of rows without indexing
items.fix: Use BallTree/KDTree in sklearn for moderate d, or approximate nearest-neighbor libraries (FAISS, Annoy) for large n.
items.why: Linear scan per query does not meet latency SLAs at production scale.
items.example: 10M × 128-d vectors: batch FAISS index vs O(10M) distance loop per request.
